Politics.headline: Trump says U.S. is negotiating end to war with Iran
Politics.detail: Trump said he had told the U.S. military to postpone strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure for five days, while talks proceed. The president's announcement sent markets soaring and energy prices diving, as investors bet Iran's blockade of a key shipping chokepoint could soon end.

Politics.headline: Starmer says 'every lever' will be explored to ease rising costs of living from Iran conflict
Politics.detail: Keir Starmer stated that all options will be considered to alleviate the increasing cost of living resulting from the Iran conflict.

Politics.headline: 'Noisy voices': Jones responds to critics of fisheries bill
Politics.detail: Shane Jones has labeled critics of the fisheries amendment bill as 'noisy voices'. The bill is scheduled for its first reading on Tuesday.

Politics.headline: Dual citizens weigh Trump, taxes in decision to renounce U.S. citizenship
Politics.detail: Dual citizens are considering Trump's policies and taxes when deciding whether to renounce their U.S. citizenship.

Politics.headline: Trump to deploy ICE agents to airports Monday
Politics.detail: President Trump said Sunday he will send ICE agents to U.S. airports starting Monday to assist TSA officers who have been working without pay for more than five weeks during a partial Homeland Security shutdown. The move thrusts the agency that sparked the shutdown with its conduct in Minnesota into the nation's airports to deal with the consequences.

Finance & Economy.headline: Global economy faces 'major, major threat' from Iran war, says IEA head
Finance & Economy.detail: The head of the International Energy Agency stated that the global economy is under a significant threat due to the ongoing Iran war, with the crisis impacting oil and gas markets more severely than previous major disruptions. The IEA chief also mentioned that 40 energy assets in nine countries across the region were severely or partially damaged.

Finance & Economy.headline: Recession risks rise for the global economy
Finance & Economy.detail: The International Energy Agency (IEA) has warned that recession risks are rising due to what they call the greatest shock to the global energy sector, stemming from the US and Israeli military campaign against Iran. Iran has vowed to try to cause the price of oil to soar to US$200 a barrel if the war with the US and Israel continues.

Finance & Economy.headline: Japanese economy faces growing pressure from Mideast conflict; producers in crisis
Finance & Economy.detail: Despite a severe energy shock caused by the Strait of Hormuz closure, the Bank of Japan (BoJ) has maintained a largely optimistic outlook, though the ongoing Middle East conflict has roiled global financial and capital markets, with oil prices rising over 50% since the conflict began. Estimated GDP growth for 2026 was lowered to 0.8% year on year from 1.0%, while the average annual CPI forecast was revised up by 0.2 percentage points to 2.2%.

Finance & Economy.headline: Economics vs geopolitics: does the wolf finally bite?
Finance & Economy.detail: The Iran war is heavily disrupting commodity and financial markets, causing shockwaves across the global economy, with oil prices rising from approximately $US70 to $US110 a barrel. This shock has again demonstrated that the US dollar remains a pre-eminent safe-haven currency.

Finance & Economy.headline: Finance min.: State recovers assets worth EGP 1.2 bln, additional EGP 1.4 bln treasury revenues
Finance & Economy.detail: Egypt's Minister of Finance, Ahmed Kouchouk, announced that the State recovered assets worth EGP 1.2 billion and collected additional resources for the treasury totaling EGP 1.4 billion through the "authority of management and disposal of recovered and seized funds". The ministry is reportedly working to create new investment opportunities for the private sector through partnerships for economic and real estate development.

Technology.headline: Palantir trial plugs into UK financial watchdog's data trove
Technology.detail: US analytics firm handed access to sensitive intel, raising yet more questions about vendor lock-in.

Technology.headline: Reddit Could Soon Require Face ID to Prove You're Not a Bot
Technology.detail: Reddit CEO Steve Huffman said Face ID, Touch ID, and other forms of passkey technology were the 'most lightweight way' to ensure all Reddit users were human.

Technology.headline: Meta's Zuckerberg developing AI agent to help with his CEO duties: Report
Technology.detail: Mark Zuckerberg is reportedly developing an AI agent to assist him with his responsibilities as CEO of Meta.

Technology.headline: Intel's Core Ultra 270K, 250K Plus are an appeal to cash-strapped PC enthusiasts
Technology.detail: Review of Intel's Core Ultra 270K and 250K Plus processors suggests they offer more cores, higher clocks, and lower prices, making them appealing to PC enthusiasts.

Technology.headline: US chip testing firm shrugged off ransomware hit as minor - then came the data leak
Technology.detail: Trio-Tech International initially said hack wasn't 'material,' but then stolen data was published.

Science.headline: Scientists discover hidden 'death switch' in the brain that may be driving Alzheimer's disease
Science.detail: A toxic pairing of two proteins triggers the destruction of brain cells, but researchers have found a way to turn it off in mice.

Science.headline: New blood test could change the outlook for pancreatic cancer by catching it much earlier
Science.detail: Researchers identified two previously unknown proteins in the blood that, when combined with existing markers, can detect pancreatic cancer earlier than ever before.

Science.headline: Most microplastics arrive through the air, settling onto treetops before being washed to the forest floor
Science.detail: A new study reveals the primary pathway for microplastic contamination in forests, highlighting the role of atmospheric deposition.

Science.headline: World Meteorological Day: Ocean Heat Breaks Record, Scientists Warn
Science.detail: Scientists are warning that ocean heat has broken records, raising concerns about the environment.

Science.headline: Indigenous wisdom can guide Indonesia's efforts to build a sustainable ocean economy
Science.detail: A report suggests that incorporating indigenous knowledge can help Indonesia develop a more sustainable ocean economy.

Health.headline: Weight Loss Drug Ozempic Cuts Depression, Anxiety, and Addiction Risk
Health.detail: GLP-1 medications like semaglutide (Ozempic) may offer unexpected mental health benefits alongside weight loss, with a large study finding major drops in depression, anxiety, and psychiatric-related hospital visits among users.

Health.headline: New Pill Cuts “Bad” Cholesterol by 60% in Major Trial
Health.detail: A new pill has demonstrated the ability to cut “bad” cholesterol by 60% in a major trial, offering a potential new treatment option for individuals struggling with high cholesterol.

Health.headline: Study reveals earlier heat risk thresholds for vulnerable seniors
Health.detail: A new study has identified earlier heat risk thresholds for vulnerable seniors, highlighting the need for proactive measures to protect this population during periods of rising temperatures.

Health.headline: AI-assisted decision support system improves stroke care and long-term outcomes.
Health.detail: An AI-assisted decision support system has been shown to improve stroke care and long-term outcomes, suggesting a promising role for artificial intelligence in clinical settings.

Health.headline: Initial tests find lead in children's fast-fashion clothing
Health.detail: Initial tests have discovered lead in children's fast-fashion clothing, raising concerns about potential health risks associated with these products.

Sports.headline: Iowa hits clutch 3-pointer in closing seconds to stun Florida
Sports.detail: Defending champion Florida was eliminated from March Madness by a tougher, more physical Iowa team after a late 3-pointer sealed the victory.

Sports.headline: Dylan Darling hits driving layup at buzzer, St. John's beats Kansas 67-65 in March Madness thriller
Sports.detail: Dylan Darling's layup at the buzzer secured a thrilling 67-65 victory for St. John's against Kansas in the NCAA Tournament.

Sports.headline: Tyler Reddick wins at Darlington in 4th NASCAR win this season for Michael Jordan's team
Sports.detail: Tyler Reddick overcame challenges to win at Darlington, marking his 4th NASCAR win this season for Michael Jordan's team.

Sports.headline: Manchester City's League Cup glory adds twist to English Premier League title race
Sports.detail: Manchester City's League Cup victory has added an interesting dimension to the ongoing English Premier League title race.

Sports.headline: Lakers star Luka Doncic clear to play at Detroit after the NBA rescinds his 16th technical foul.
Sports.detail: Luka Doncic is cleared to play after the NBA rescinds his 16th technical foul.

Entertainment.headline: Chuck Norris Dead at 86
Entertainment.detail: Martial arts master and actor Chuck Norris, whose toughness became internet lore, has passed away at the age of 86.

Entertainment.headline: Justin Timberlake's DWI Arrest Video is Released Despite His Attempt to Block It
Entertainment.detail: Footage of Justin Timberlake's DWI arrest has been released, despite his efforts to keep it private.

Entertainment.headline: Taylor Frankie Paul Temporarily Loses Custody of Son With Dakota Mortenson After Brutal Fight Video
Entertainment.detail: Taylor Frankie Paul has temporarily lost custody of her son after a video surfaced of a brutal fight with Dakota Mortenson.

Entertainment.headline: US man pleads guilty to defrauding music streamers out of millions using AI
Entertainment.detail: A man in the US has pleaded guilty to using AI to defraud music streamers out of millions.

Entertainment.headline: Barry Keoghan says online abuse means he 'doesn't want to go outside' any more
Entertainment.detail: Barry Keoghan has stated that he no longer wants to leave his home due to online abuse.

Crime & Justice.headline: First Edition newsletter: Monday briefing: What a new Guardian podcast reveals about the US justice system
Crime & Justice.detail: Off Duty revisits the conviction of Alexander Villa, raising troubling questions about how it was built.

Crime & Justice.headline: Boston officer charged with manslaughter after shooting carjacking suspect as video disputes account
Crime & Justice.detail: Boston police officer Nicholas O'Malley charged with voluntary manslaughter after allegedly shooting suspected carjacker Stephenson King during incident.

Crime & Justice.headline: Bridgewater teen in court after threats to schools in N.S. and Manitoba
Crime & Justice.detail: A 15-year-old girl from Bridgewater, N.S., is scheduled to appear in court for a bail hearing Monday after alleged threats to schools in Nova Scotia and Manitoba.

Crime & Justice.headline: Arkansas police arrest Kendra Duggar on child abuse charges
Crime & Justice.detail: Kendra Duggar was arrested and charged with child abuse.

Crime & Justice.headline: Denver removes César Chavez bust after sexual abuse allegations
Crime & Justice.detail: Denver has removed a bust of César Chavez following sexual abuse allegations.

Education.headline: Education minister details school renovations
Education.detail: The Ministry of Education in Bermuda was allocated $156.5 million for the 2026-27 Budget, with public schools seeing a 71 per cent boost in capital spending for improvements to their buildings and infrastructure. The Budget also contained a $14-million package for the Ministry of Education to upgrade facilities and invest in new technology at the schools and the Bermuda College.

Education.headline: Final School of Education & Human Sciences dean candidate to present March 23
Education.detail: Joshua Newman, associate dean for research and Sara Lavinia de Keni Endowed Professor at Florida State University's Anne Spencer Daves College of Education, Health, and Human Sciences, will be the fourth and final dean candidate for the University of Kansas School of Education & Human Sciences. His public presentation detailing his vision and strategies for the school will take place 3:45-4:45 p.m. March 23 in 150 Joseph R. Pearson Hall.

Education.headline: Florida limits on race discussions raise concerns after racist chat emerges
Education.detail: Florida's push to restrict discussion about race relations in the state's classrooms is creating discomfort at Florida International University after a group of students was found participating in a racist group chat that gained statewide and national attention. The Miami Herald found an atmosphere described as constrained by state rules that have led to the elimination of programs and courses that include equity, diversity and inclusion.

Education.headline: Gretchen Whitmer's failed literacy test
Education.detail: An opinion piece in the Washington Post discusses Michigan Governor Gretchen Whitmer's efforts to improve literacy rates, while criticizing her decision to eliminate the requirement for third-grade students to be held back if they can't read at grade level. The author argues that this policy undermines the state's progress in addressing literacy challenges.

Education.headline: EU strengthens aid for Lebanon as the crisis deepens
Education.detail: The EU is adapting its education response to ensure learning continues in Lebanon, by enabling remote classes for 50,000 students and providing digital training tools for 8,000 teachers. This expanded support will guarantee integrated education and child protection services in shelters and temporary learning spaces, reaching a total of 150,000 children and young people in need through both in-person and community-based programmes.

Lifestyle.headline: Hilton signs agreement with Yotel to expand lifestyle hotel portfolio
Lifestyle.detail: Hilton has entered into an agreement with Yotel to expand its global hotel portfolio, adding a brand focused on compact, technology-driven accommodation in urban markets. The agreement allows Yotel properties to access Hilton's distribution and technology platforms while maintaining their existing service model.

Lifestyle.headline: As demand for GLP-1 pills and shots surges, healthy habits are still key
Lifestyle.detail: More Americans are turning to anti-obesity drugs to lose weight and boost health, but experts caution that medication alone isn't the answer. Lifestyle changes, including a healthy diet, exercise, adequate sleep and stress management, are needed to reap the biggest benefits from GLP-1 drugs.

Lifestyle.headline: Expecting dads should cultivate healthy routines before childbirth
Lifestyle.detail: A recent study has found that men's lifestyle choices such as smoking, drinking, body weight, diet and mental health can influence their children even from the prepregnancy stage; therefore, men need to manage their health well before becoming fathers. The study also highlighted that a partner's participation and support during pregnancy can influence the pregnancy process itself.

Lifestyle.headline: Actors Guild offers free health checks in FCT, tasks actors on healthy lifestyle
Lifestyle.detail: The Actors Guild of Nigeria (AGN) has offered free health checks for Nollywood actors in the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) as part of the 2026 annual “AGN Health Campaign and Walk for Life” initiative. The FCT Mayor of AGN, Amb. Mercy Salma Aminu, urged actors and other industry players to prioritize a healthy lifestyle to avoid imminent health breakdown.

Lifestyle.headline: Cigarette smoking in America plummets to historic single-digit low, new study finds
Lifestyle.detail: A recent study reveals that cigarette smoking in America has dropped to a historic single-digit low. This indicates a significant shift in lifestyle habits related to health in the United States.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Don't blame Trump's stupid war on Israel
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Jewish state, as usual, makes a handy fall guy. The president might look in the mirror instead.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Gretchen Whitmer's failed literacy test
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Molly Macek discusses Gretchen Whitmer's policies in an opinion piece.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: A call from Filipino professionals: We need leaders with empathy
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece emphasizing the need for empathetic leaders in the Philippines.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Critical need to combat bullying
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial highlights the importance of addressing and preventing bullying.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Max Boot: Don't blame Trump's stupid war on Israel
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Max Boot argues that Israel should not be blamed for the ongoing conflict, suggesting the president should take responsibility.

World.headline: US says Iran war may last longer than planned: Report
World.detail: The U.S. has informed Israel that the war against Iran is likely to last longer than originally anticipated, with operations aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz expected to take several more weeks; Washington's goal is to eliminate Iran's influence over global energy prices.

World.headline: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 23 March: Civilians bear brunt of ongoing war
World.detail: The war in the Middle East is well into its fourth week and the humanitarian emergency it has sparked continues to be the focus of international attention, along with the energy crisis caused by attacks on shipping in key Strait of Hormuz and other oil and gas infrastructure in the Gulf region.

World.headline: 2 pilots killed as plane and fire-rescue truck collide at LaGuardia Airport
World.detail: Two pilots were killed and dozens of people injured at New York's LaGuardia Airport late Sunday night when an arriving Air Canada Express plane and fire and rescue vehicle collided, authorities said.

World.headline: U.K: Ambulances set on fire in anti-Semitic attack
World.detail: Four ambulances belonging to a Jewish emergency service have been firebombed in an anti-Semitic attack outside a synagogue in London. The Metropolitan Police confirmed it was treating the suspected arson attack as an anti-Semitic hate crime.

World.headline: EU hosts high-level conference to mark 1 year of its Preparedness Union Strategy
World.detail: The European Union opened a two-day conference in Brussels to mark the first anniversary of its Preparedness Union Strategy, bringing together government representatives, civil protection experts, military representatives, private sector leaders and partners from across Europe to take stock of progress and chart the path ahead.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe Accelerates Muzarabani Oil Project to Cut Fuel Imports
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwean Government is increasing efforts to advance the Muzarabani oil and gas project, viewing it as vital for reducing reliance on fuel imports and protecting the economy from global energy shocks. Officials consider it a key step to stabilize energy supply and support economic growth.

Zimbabwe.headline: Parirenyatwa Nurses Protest, Demand a Salary Review Amid Rising Fuel and Transport Costs
Zimbabwe.detail: Nurses at Parirenyatwa Hospital are protesting, demanding a salary review in light of increasing fuel and transport costs. The protests highlight the impact of rising costs on essential workers.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe detains top opponent of changes extending president's rule
Zimbabwe.detail: Tendai Biti, a former finance minister and leading opponent of constitutional amendments that could extend President Mnangagwa's rule, has been detained. The proposed amendments would allow the president to be elected by Parliament and extend presidential terms.

Zimbabwe.headline: Schools To Refund ZIMSEC Exam Fees For Candidates Exceeding Subject Limits
Zimbabwe.detail: Schools are mandated to reimburse ZIMSEC exam fees to candidates who surpassed the stipulated subject limits. This directive ensures compliance with exam regulations.

Zimbabwe.headline: Govt activates emergency measures to protect medicine supply chain
Zimbabwe.detail: The government is implementing emergency measures to safeguard the medicine supply chain. This action aims to ensure the continuous availability of essential medicines.
